capturedCommands.scope: Runs the probe source in @lynx-js/testing-environment with jsdom. It proves a page/view/text tree after either main-first or background-first startup, cancellation before main readiness, one acknowledged tap-driven background update, request-correlated idempotent terminal teardown while a mount acknowledgement is pending or after an outcome-ambiguous mount/update transport fault, cleanup when root append throws before or after mutation or removal mutates and then throws, validation-before-mutation for invalid, structurally stale, and conflicting-parent batches, and that background state remains at its last acknowledged value while a commit is pending or rejected. It does not execute the encoded .lynx.bundle.

Checks existing build outputs, decodes the native bundle, confirms distinct main/background programs without thread-global branches, checks the isolated lock graph, and searches for ReactLynx/Preact. Successful decoding is not execution by a Lynx engine.

capturedCommands.command: Lynx Web browser smoke for / and /?imperative

result.transportedConsoleError: Uncaught TypeError: Failed to execute 'observe' on 'MutationObserver': parameter 1 is not of type 'Node'.
result.imperativeConsoleError: Uncaught TypeError: Failed to execute 'observe' on 'MutationObserver': parameter 1 is not of type 'Node'.

capturedCommands.scope: Runs the pinned @lynx-js/web-core@0.22.2 client in Chromium against fresh tabs. Both the transported probe and direct-PAPI control fail first with the same MutationObserver error before exposing Count: 0; the transported path additionally reports that the Web context does not implement postMessage. The shared bootstrap failure is not attributed to the background protocol without further engine diagnostics.

capturedCommands.command: command -v adb; xcrun simctl list devices available; locate Lynx Explorer
capturedCommands.scope: Records why native execution cannot run on the capture host. Missing device lanes remain blocking gates.

evidence: neither decoded program branches on thread globals
no ReactLynx or Preact marker was found
the unused imperative background program is empty
no ReactLynx or Preact marker was found
decoded programs use only their respective JS/core context and no thread globals
no ReactLynx or Preact marker was found
no ReactLynx or Preact marker was found

transportedNativeBundleBytes.qualification: Single local production artifact; no regression budget is ratified.

transportedWebBundleBytes.qualification: Single local production artifact; no regression budget is ratified.

imperativeNativeBundleBytes.qualification: Direct-PAPI control artifact only; no runtime timing or regression budget is ratified.

imperativeWebBundleBytes.qualification: Direct-PAPI control artifact only; no runtime timing or regression budget is ratified.

firstPaint.reason: No official engine execution or instrumentation was performed.

backgroundUpdate.reason: The source-level testing environment observed Count: 0 to Count: 1 after ACK; no timing was measured.

patchBytes.reason: The transport batch was not instrumented for serialized byte size.

commitFlush.reason: The receiver has one flush boundary after applying a validated batch, and the invalid-batch test proves zero flushes. The accepted-path flush count is not yet a separately captured native benchmark.

teardown.reason: Source-level testing removes the page child and listeners; no engine teardown timing or lifecycle callback was captured.

reactLynxComparison.reason: No same-host ReactLynx baseline was captured.

imperativePapiComparison.reason: The direct-PAPI native/Web controls are 3637/3286 bytes versus 15767/15189 bytes for the transported probe. Both Web controls fail before rendering, and no engine timing is available.

gates.evidence: Seventeen Node tests passed using @lynx-js/testing-environment@0.3.0, including both startup orders, cancellation before main readiness, request-correlated idempotent terminal teardown after outcome-ambiguous faults, cleanup when page-root append throws before or after mutation or removal mutates and then throws, and rejection of conflicting parent links before native mutation.
The existing production .lynx.bundle decodes with explicit main/background programs, no thread-global branch, and engine-version 3.9.

gates.reason: The source modules ran in the official JavaScript testing environment; the encoded .lynx.bundle itself was only decoded.

Fresh Chromium runs of both transported and direct-PAPI Web bundles exposed no Count: 0 tree and first reported MutationObserver.observe receiving a non-Node target. The transported path also reported that Web postMessage is unimplemented.
No Lynx Explorer installation was available on the capture host.
adb was not installed and no Android emulator or device execution was available.
xcrun was installed, but simctl reported no available simulator devices and Explorer was not installed.
The working test injection uses lynxCoreInject.tt.publishEvent, which is absent from the selected public type packages.
ReactLynx uses injected onAppReload and callDestroyLifetimeFun hooks. A framework-neutral replacement has not been confirmed across engines.
All 213 upstream test source files are classified, but dynamic Vitest/cargo case expansion has not been captured and classified case-by-case.
ReactLynx and imperative-PAPI first-paint/update/teardown timing, patch-byte, and flush baselines require a working real engine lane.

milestoneExit.decision: Do not begin the production renderer port until the public-hook blockers are resolved and the exact bundle passes the required engine matrix.
